items.title: Those who target schools, children with impunity must be held into account: India tells UNSC
items.summary: The United Nations verified 38,558 grave violations, which affected 24,174 children in 2025 (15,493 boys, 7,990 girls, 691 sex unknown), the highest number of children affected by grave violations since the beginning of the mandate

items.title: Venezuela earthquake LIVE updates: World sends rescuers, aid as Venezuela reels from quakes
items.summary: Senior military official lands in Venezuela to oversee U.S. quake response; The number of dead and injured is expected to rise, with thousands reported missing after the 7.2- and 7.5-magnitude earthquakes

items.title: Omega block: What is the weather phenomenon driving Europe’s heatwave
items.summary: An omega block is a stalled, Ω-shaped wind pattern that confines air within it, preventing ventilation. This leads to a build-up of heat, resulting in oppressive conditions

items.summary: Regional governor Dmitry ‌Milyayev said an industrial facility in Novomoskovsk, 200 km (125 miles) south of ‌Moscow, had been damaged

items.title: U.S. Supreme Court’s ruling to end protections for Haitian, Syrian immigrants could have broader impact
items.summary: The Supreme Court ’s decision allowing President Donald Trump’s administration to end temporary legal protections for Haitian and Syrian immigrants may affect people from many other countries

items.title: Japan cancels 120 flights as twin storms approach
items.summary: Japan Airlines and All Nippon Airways cancelled a total of 120 flights to and from the southern regions of Okinawa and Kagoshima

items.title: Russia says shot down 660 Ukrainian drones overnight: Defence Ministry
items.summary: Drones were destroyed over more than a dozen regions, including the Russian capital and the annexed Crimean Peninsula, the Black Sea, and the Sea of Azov, the Ministry said on Russia's state-run Max platform.

items.title: Philippines temporarily blocks gaming app used by suspect in deadly school shooting
items.summary: Three students were killed, and 20 others were wounded when the two suspects, aged 14 and 15 and armed with a handgun each, opened fire June 22 at the San Jose National High School in central Tacloban city

items.summary: The number of dead and injured is expected to rise with thousands reported missing

items.summary: Tarique Rahman is on a three-day trip to China, which ‌began on June 24, his first visit to the country since he became Prime Minister

items.title: Afghanistan's Taliban government imposes smartphone ban on government officials
items.summary: Rights advocates said smartphones remain ⁠one of the few tools Afghans use to access education, document abuses, communicate privately and reach uncensored information

items.title: Pakistan says Iran-U.S. talks expected to resume next week
items.summary: In a statement issued late on June 24, 2026, Pakistan's foreign office spokesperson Tahir Andrabi said “parties are on the table” and that the process was going on

items.title: WhatsApp still barely makes Meta any money. Can Kunal Shah change that?
items.summary: This week, Meta named Kunal Shah, founder of Indian fintech CRED, as WhatsApp’s new global head, succeeding Will Cathcart after nearly seven years at the helm

items.title: Trump allies defend him to Israelis anxious over Iran deal
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli relationship has been on a roller coaster, from the ​early confidence they shared after their joint attack on Iran to public disagreements between Donald Trump and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu over how ⁠to end the four-month-old war

items.title: UN agency says it pauses Hormuz ship evacuation initiative after vessel attacked
items.summary: The head of the International Maritime Organization said the plan to move stranded ships out of the Persian Gulf through the strait will be on hold until the agency can confirm safety guarantees for the ships on the evacuation listt

items.title: Venezuela earthquake: On the streets and in hospitals, Venezuelans scramble to save lives
items.summary: Residents of La Guaira, a popular spot ‌for ⁠beachgoers and the worst-hit city in the country, and Moron, near the quakes’ epicenter, were scrambling ⁠amid limited official help

items.title: Venezuela quake response requires ‘massive collective effort’: UN aid chief
items.summary: “We are fully mobilised to support the people of Venezuela,” UN Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator Tom Fletcher said in a statement.

items.title: Venezuela declares state of emergency after powerful twin earthquakes; Maiquetia airport closed

items.title: Venezuela’s deadly history of earthquakes — and how they affected its politics
items.summary: As the 1812 earthquake during the War of Independence illustrated, Venezuela is no stranger to natural disasters being bound up with moments of political upheaval

items.title: Venezuela earthquake: Venezuelans in U.S. rush to send aid to earthquake victims, but Caracas airport is closed
items.summary: In Washington, the Trump administration said it’s sending $150 million to support relief efforts by aid groups and the United Nations, according to a U.S. State Department news release

items.title: Washington Reflecting Pool now under surveillance as Trump blames vandals for green algae
items.summary: National Guard troops ‌patrol the area around the roughly 2,000-foot-long (610-meter) basin on Washington’s National Mall in groups of threes and fours

items.title: North Korean leader Kim oversees weapons tests, KCNA reports
items.summary: The tests ‌involved an upgraded version ‌of ⁠a 240-mm, 24-tube multiple rocket ⁠launcher system, tactical ballistic missiles, and a 155-mm self-propelled howitzer

items.title: Paris sets up ban on drinking alcohol in public to deal with heatwave
items.summary: There would also be ⁠a ban on the ⁠sale of alcoholic products from Friday ‌(June 26, 2026) evening onwards in Paris

items.title: King Charles III will not live in Buckingham Palace after completion of costly refurbishment
items.summary: The decision was announced Thursday (June 25, 2026) during a briefing on royal finances at which Charles became the first British monarch to reveal the taxes he paid to the government

items.title: U.S. House Speaker Johnson tries to patch things up between Trump and Congress
items.summary: Mr. Trump and Mr. Johnson were expected to discuss a legislative path forward for the SAVE ‌America Act and rescheduling the signing ceremony for the housing bill. Mr. Johnson is the top Republican in the House

items.title: Venezuela earthquake updates on June 25, 2026: International aid heads to Venezuela after deadly earthquake
items.summary: The powerful back-to-back earthquakes killed at least 164 people and injured at least 971, Venezuela's Acting President Delcy Rodríguez said, as communities across the South American country sustained damage

items.title: Strait of Hormuz: How months of closure creates  challenges for ships
items.summary: Even if shipping traffic resumes through the Strait of Hormuz, hundreds of vessels that have spent weeks or months waiting at anchor must first overcome a range of technical, operational and human challenges before returning to normal trade

items.title: Fresh dispute emerges over Strait of Hormuz transit routes despite MoU
items.summary: Naval Force of IRGC warns vessels transiting the strait must use only routes officially designated by Iran

items.title: Watch: Passport, Aadhaar, voter ID — what proves your citizenship? | Above the Fold | 25.06.2026
items.summary: In this episode, we examine the debate over whether a passport proves citizenship and what documents actually establish citizenship rights. We also cover the deadly earthquakes in Venezuela, NCERT’s new Class IX chapter on the Emergency and the political reactions it has sparked, and more.

items.title: Tarique Rahman in China: Bangladesh discusses Teesta project in Beijing
items.summary: China has said the Teesta River Comprehensive Management and Restoration Project would include dredging of more than 100 km of the river, which would allow better management of the river’s water and prevent erosion

items.title: FATF asks Myanmar to act against cyber scam threats, protect trafficking victims
items.summary: The FATF noted that fraud and cyber scam activities in Myanmar remained extensive and presented significant illicit finance risks, despite steps taken to tackle such operations

items.title: Cargo ship reports suspected attack while crossing Hormuz
items.summary: The vessel hit was the Singapore-flagged container ship Ever Lovely, according to British maritime risk management group Vanguard and three maritime security sources

items.title: China defends patrols east of Taiwan after three European nations raise alarm
items.summary: While Britain, France and Germany said the activity endangered regional stability, a Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson said China’s law-enforcement and patrol activities were aimed at maintaining regional stability and maritime order

items.title: Macron hosts Meloni for Riviera talks after Trump rift
items.summary: Relations have often been strained between Mr. Macron, a pro-European centrist, and Ms. Meloni, who leads a coalition of right and far-right parties

items.title: Russia denies pressuring Belarus to widen Ukraine conflict; Minsk blames West
items.summary: The Wall Street Journal reported ‌that Russia wanted to use Belarus as a springboard to step up ⁠attacks on Ukraine, and that Moscow was threatening to cut financial support if it did not agree

items.title: Poland, Ukraine hold key Gdansk conference without Zelenskyy
items.summary: Polish PM Donald Tusk called on the West to believe a post-war Ukraine will “rise from the ruins” like his native city Gdansk, largely destroyed during World War II, as he tried to dampen the diplomatic spat with Kyiv

items.title: French Court orders TotalEnergies to account for clients’ emissions
items.summary: The four NGOs that took TotalEnergies to court said the law’s reference to prevention of environmental risks encompasses both local pollution and climate change.

items.title: PACOM, the deeper meaning behind a dropped prefix
items.summary: The U.S.’s reversion from INDOPACOM to PACOM suggests that its regional shifts run deeper than a simple name change

items.title: Why is Europe baking this summer? | Explained
items.summary: In its second major heatwave in two months, Europe has been recording a steep increase in temperatures since mid-June

items.title: Iraq urges OPEC to raise its quota
items.summary: OPEC has "responded by launching a process to reassess" its members states' capacities, the Ministry said

items.title: India’s envoy to Bangladesh Dinesh Trivedi assumes office, announces resumption of travel visas
items.summary: Visa applications can be submitted starting June 28, Mr. Trivedi said after presenting his credentials to Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin; Union govt. accords Mr. Trivedi the status of a Union Minister for ceremonial functions

items.title: Consider Teesta water sharing deal under ‘current circumstances’: Bangladesh FM post BJP’s Bengal win
items.summary: Mamata percieved as hurdle in Teesta talks; ahead of Beijing trip, Bangladesh FM says, “Of course, Teesta will be discussed with China”; Dhaka demands greater share of Teesta waters

items.title: Trump says it may never be known who was at fault for strike on girls' school in Iran

items.summary: When I arrived, there was a poster with my face and the word ‘Welcome’ every few feet along the road; when I left, the posters showed the same image, but now read ‘Thank you for visiting’, says Giorgia Meloni

items.title: Any deal with Iran will ensure security of Gulf allies: Rubio
items.summary: The U.S. Secretary of State’s three-day tour of ​the Gulf is the first high-level diplomatic mission ‌since the U.S.-Iran framework agreement last week to end the conflict

items.title: Congo’s Ebola outbreak has most cases in first month of any African outbreak, WHO says
items.summary: The Bundibugyo outbreak in the Democratic Republic ‌of Congo that has infected over 1,000 people and killed 267 ‌was detected late and experts say ‌the ⁠virus had already been circulating ⁠for months before it was officially declared on May 15

items.title: Jaishankar calls for closer cooperation with South Korea at Jeju Forum
items.summary: Mr. Jaishankar also called for “greater capacity and opportunities” for the Global South to create new factors of growth, while highlighting that “the interests of a few” nations are being “openly prioritised” - without naming any country

items.summary: From India’s Russian oil purchases to legal battles in Washington, the road to India-U.S. trade deal has become increasingly uncertain, and The Hindu explains why

items.summary: Acting Venezuela President warned the toll was expected to rise as rescuers searched collapsed buildings and emergency crews reached devastated areas

items.summary: The statement by Revolutionary Guards did not elaborate on denounced routes but it appeared to be a response to an announcement overnight of a temporary corridor by Oman, which also borders the strait

items.summary: In ‌coordination with the International Maritime Organization, Oman established temporary maritime corridors to help ships leave the area safely amid heightened security risks

items.title: India, U.S. note ‘substantial progress’ on trade deal talks as U.S. Trade Representative Greer ends two-day visit to Delhi
items.summary: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer held multiple meetings with Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal; however, the two sides have not yet been able to announce a deadline by which the trade deals will be finalised

items.title: U.S. Senate Republicans reject War Powers resolution after Trump berates them
items.summary: It’s unclear whether the move will be enough to appease Mr. Trump, who had called the Republicans “losers” for voting against his war and had called Sen. Bill Cassidy a “lunatic” at the lunch after their tense exchange

items.title: Europe heatwave is 'price to pay for fossil fuel pollution': UN climate chief
items.summary: Until humanity stops burning colossal amounts of coal, oil and gas, extreme heat will keep getting worse, says UN climate chief Simon Stiell
